title: [phase:critique] [family:sidecar-review] [provider_hint:subagent] Review remaining-task implementation only: auth/rate-limit/client UX + scripts/smoke-happy-login.js and scripts/smoke-happy-api.js. Return prioritized real findings with file refs; if none, state no findings plus residual risks.
summary_text: 1. P1: The requested happy-path smoke entrypoints are missing, so this implementation has no runnable login/API smoke validation at all. I could not review `scripts/smoke-happy-login.js` or `scripts/smoke-happy-api.js` because neither file exists in [scripts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts). 2. P1: HTTP 429 is still treated as a generic request failure, not a rate-limit condition, and the client keeps polling at the same cadence while throttled. `endpointError()` only special-cases `401` and `503`, `classifyEndpointFailure()` only maps auth/service, and the app keeps the fixed 15s/5s polling loops regardless of throttle state. That means a rate-limited backend will surface the wrong UX and may be hammered continuously instead of backing off. Refs: [happy-endpoint-client.js#L18](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L18), [happy-endpoint-client.js#L88](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L88), [happy-app-state.js#L56](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-app-state.js#L56), [app.js#L1058](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/app.js#L1058) 3. P2: “Live session disabled” is effectively impossible, because `liveSessionOpenUrl` defaults to `"/cockpit"` and `enabled` is computed from `liveSessionEnabled || liveSessionOpenUrl`. In practice, even an explicitly disabled config still looks enabled and `launchLiveSession()` will navigate away to `/cockpit`. Refs: [happy-runtime-config.js#L45](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/config/happy-runtime-config.js#L45), [app.js#L229](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/app.js#L229), [app.js#L879](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/app.js#L879) 4. P2: A failed `/events` poll followed by a successful `/state` fallback still leaves the sync outcome marked degraded, so the UI can claim “auth stale” or “service wait” even after a fresh authoritative snapshot was loaded. `hydrateFromRemote()` sets success, then `syncRemoteStateInternal()` overwrites it back to degraded based on the earlier event-feed failure; `renderSyncStatus()` uses that stale error state for the banner. Refs: [happy-app-state.js#L398](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-app-state.js#L398), [happy-app-state.js#L490](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-app-state.js#L490), [app.js#L121](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/app.js#L121) Residual risk: the targeted tests I ran passed, but they do not cover the missing smoke scripts, 429 behavior, live-disabled routing, or the `/events`-fails-then-`/state`-succeeds case. Refs: [test-behavior-contract.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-behavior-contract.js), [test-endpoint-client.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-endpoint-client.js), [test-view-contract.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-view-contract.sh)
